questions.scenario: The failure-rate alert for `devpolaris-orders-api` currently sends to `ag-orders-oncall`. A platform team also wants every checkout alert. What should the team decide before adding them?

options.text: Whether the platform team owns an action for this alert, or whether they only need dashboard visibility.
Whether the platform team has a shorter email address, because shorter receivers reduce alert noise.
Whether the alert can be made critical so everyone treats it seriously.
Whether the action group name appears in Application Insights traces.

questions.explanation: Action groups should match ownership. Adding receivers who cannot act increases noise without improving response.

questions.scenario: A latency alert triggers every morning during a known batch window, but users are not affected. Which changes could improve the alert without hiding real incidents?

options.text: Adjust the threshold or evaluation window to match user-impacting latency.
Add a dashboard note or separate batch metric so the pattern is visible.
Review whether the alert should use failed requests, duration, or both.
Delete the action group so no one is notified about any checkout incident.

questions.explanation: Alert tuning should reduce noise while preserving important signals. Removing all receivers hides real incidents instead of improving the alert.
